metadata.note: Questions 101-300. No overlap with previous Infosys sets or first 100 Microsoft questions.

questions.title: Median of Two Sorted Arrays (Microsoft hard favorite)
questions.problem_statement: Find median of two sorted arrays in O(log(min(m,n))) time.
options: A) Merge arrays and pick median O(m+n)
B) Binary search on smaller array partitioning
C) Use two heaps (median from data stream) O((m+n) log(m+n))
D) Both B and advanced approach
questions.correct_answer: D) Both B and advanced approach
questions.explanation: Partition arrays such that left parts <= right parts; adjust partition via binary search.

questions.title: Longest Palindromic Substring (Microsoft classic)
questions.problem_statement: Find longest palindromic substring in given string.
options: B) DP O(n^2) time and space
questions.explanation: Manacher's is optimal O(n); expand around center is simpler O(n^2).

questions.title: Palindromic Substrings (count palindromes)
questions.problem_statement: Count number of palindromic substrings.
options: A) Expand around center, count each
questions.explanation: Expand around center counts odd and even palindromes in O(n^2).

questions.title: Longest Valid Parentheses (Microsoft onsite)
questions.problem_statement: Length of longest valid parentheses substring.
options: A) Stack storing indices, push -1 initially
B) DP: dp[i] = dp[i-2] + 2 if s[i]==')' and s[i-1]=='('; else if s[i-1]==')' and s[i-dp[i-1]-1]=='('
D) Use two counters left/right passes
questions.explanation: Stack O(n) solution: when pop, length = i - stack.top().

questions.title: Minimum Remove to Make Valid Parentheses
questions.problem_statement: Remove minimum parentheses to make string valid.
options: A) Use stack to mark invalid indices, then build result
B) Two passes: left to right remove extra ')', right to left remove extra '('
questions.explanation: First pass remove unmatched ')', second pass remove unmatched '('.

questions.title: Decode String (Microsoft
questions.problem_statement: Decode string with pattern k[encoded].
options: A) Two stacks: count stack, string stack
questions.explanation: When '[' push current string and count to stacks; when ']' pop and repeat.

questions.title: Remove K Digits (Microsoft greedy)
questions.problem_statement: Remove k digits to make smallest number.
options: A) Greedy: use monotonic stack, remove if previous digit > current
B) Generate all combinations O(n choose k)
C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
D) Sort digits and remove largest
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.explanation: Monotonic stack ensures increasing order; pop while stack[-1] > digit and k>0.

questions.title: Next Greater Element II (circular array)
questions.problem_statement: Next greater element in circular array.
options: A) Double the array and monotonic stack
D) Two-pass: left to right then right to left
questions.explanation: Monotonic decreasing stack; iterate twice using modulo.

questions.title: Asteroid Collision (Microsoft Stack)
questions.problem_statement: Simulate asteroid collisions: + right, - left.
options: A) Stack: push asteroid, if top positive and current negative, compare sizes
B) Simulate with list and while loop
D) Sort by absolute value fails
questions.explanation: When collision (stack top >0 and curr<0), pop if abs(curr) > top, else skip.

questions.title: Largest Rectangle in Histogram (Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Find largest rectangle area in histogram.
options: A) For each bar, expand left and right O(n^2)
B) Monotonic stack: push index, when height[i] < height[stack.top()], pop and compute area
C) Both A and B, B is O(n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, B is O(n)
questions.explanation: Stack maintains increasing heights; when smaller appears, pop and calculate area.

questions.title: Maximal Rectangle (binary matrix, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Largest rectangle of 1's in binary matrix.
options: A) For each row, treat as histogram and use largest rectangle algorithm
B) DP tracking heights and left/right boundaries
questions.explanation: For each row, update heights; largest rectangle in histogram O(n) per row.

questions.title: Shortest Subarray with Sum at Least K (Monotonic deque)
questions.problem_statement: Shortest subarray with sum ≥ k (can include negative).
options: A) Prefix sum and brute force O(n^2)
B) Monotonic deque: maintain increasing prefix sums
C) Both A and B, B is O(n)
D) Sliding window fails for negatives
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, B is O(n)
questions.explanation: For each j, find i where prefix[j]-prefix[i]>=k and i minimal; deque stores candidate i.

questions.title: Sum of Subarray Minimums (monotonic stack)
questions.problem_statement: Sum of minimums of all subarrays.
options: B) Monotonic stack to find next smaller and previous smaller
C) Both A and B, B is O(n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, B is O(n)
questions.explanation: For each element, count subarrays where it is minimum using next smaller element (NSE) and previous smaller element (PSE).

questions.title: Online Stock Span (monotonic stack)
questions.problem_statement: Return span of stock price (consecutive days ≤ current price).
options: A) For each day, go back O(n^2)
B) Monotonic decreasing stack storing (price, span)
C) Both A and B, B is O(1) amortized
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, B is O(1) amortized
questions.explanation: Stack stores pairs (price, span); pop while stack top price ≤ current, accumulate span, push.

questions.title: Remove Duplicate Letters (or Smallest Subsequence of Distinct Characters)
questions.problem_statement: Remove duplicate letters to get smallest lexicographical result.
options: A) Monotonic stack with last occurrence array and visited set
C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.explanation: Stack: while stack[-1] > current and later stack[-1] exists, pop; push current.

questions.title: Maximum Frequency Stack (FreqStack)
questions.problem_statement: Implement stack that pops most frequent element (ties: closest to top).
options: A) HashMap freq and another map freq->stack of elements
B) Use heap with (freq, pushTime) O(log n)
C) Both A and B, A is O(1)
D) Single stack with frequency count
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(1)
questions.explanation: Maintain maxFreq; push: update freq, add to freqStack[freq]; pop: pop from freqStack[maxFreq].

questions.title: Design Browser History (Microsoft
questions.problem_statement: Browser history with visit, back, forward.
options: A) Two stacks: back and forward
B) Single list with current index
questions.explanation: List + index O(1); two stacks also work but need to move elements.

questions.title: Min Stack (Microsoft classic)
questions.problem_statement: Stack with push, pop, top, getMin in O(1).
options: A) Two stacks: main stack and min stack
D) Use heap with lazy deletion
questions.explanation: Min stack stores minimum up to that point; push: min = min(value, minStack.top()).

questions.title: Count Submatrices With All Ones (DP + stack or prefix sum)
questions.problem_statement: Count submatrices with all 1's.
options: A) For each row, treat as histogram, count rectangles ending at each cell using stack
B) Prefix sum and binary search fail
C) Both A and DP with stack O(n^2)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and DP with stack O(n^2)
questions.explanation: For each row, compute heights (consecutive 1's), then for each cell, use stack to count number of submatrices ending there.

questions.title: Minimum Cost Tree From Leaf Values (stack / greedy)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum cost of tree where cost = product of adjacent leaves, combine smallest.
options: A) Monotonic decreasing stack: pop smaller and combine with adjacent min
C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.explanation: Use stack to keep decreasing values; when arr[i] >= stack.top(), pop and add product with min(stack.pop(), arr[i]).

questions.title: Shortest Palindrome (hard string, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Add characters to front to make palindrome, shortest result.
options: A) Brute force: try all prefixes O(n^2)
B) KMP: reverse string, compute LPS array for s + '#' + reverse(s)
questions.explanation: Find longest palindromic prefix using KMP on s + '#' + reversed(s); answer = reverse(suffix) + s.

questions.title: Palindrome Pairs (Trie or Map, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Find all pairs of words that concatenate to palindrome.
options: A) For each word, check all others O(n^2 * L)
B) Build Trie of reversed words, check prefixes
C) Use hash map of reversed words, check empty and mirror
questions.explanation: Map approach: for each word, check if reverse exists; also check prefixes/suffixes that are palindromes.

questions.title: Valid Palindrome III (LeetCode Premium, DP)
questions.problem_statement: Can string become palindrome after removing at most k chars?
options: A) DP: lps length, check if n - lps <= k
B) Edit distance variant: min deletions to palindrome <= k
questions.explanation: Longest palindromic subsequence length; if n - lps_length <= k, true.

questions.title: Find All Good Strings (Digital DP + KMP, very hard)
questions.problem_statement: Count strings between s1 and s2 not containing evil substring.
options: A) DP on index, tight, KMP state matching evil
B) Brute force all strings O(26^n)
C) Both A and advanced automaton
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and advanced automaton
questions.explanation: Build KMP automaton for evil; DP over positions with automaton state.

questions.title: Maximum Product of Splitted Binary Tree (tree DP)
questions.problem_statement: Remove one edge, maximize product of sums of two trees.
options: A) Compute total sum, DFS compute subtree sum, track max (total-sum)*sum
questions.explanation: Post-order DFS to compute subtree sums; for each edge (parent-child), product = childSum * (total - childSum).

questions.title: Count Nodes Equal to Average of Subtree (DFS, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Count nodes where node.value == average of subtree.
options: A) DFS returning (sum, count), compare value == sum/count at each node
B) BFS compute subtree sums then compare
questions.explanation: Post-order DFS: compute sum and count for subtree; check condition.

questions.title: Kth Largest Element in a Stream (heap)
questions.problem_statement: Find kth largest in stream (add method).
options: A) Min-heap of size k, always maintain k largest
B) Max-heap of all elements O(n log n)
C) Both A and B, A is efficient
D) Sort each time O(n log n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is efficient
questions.explanation: Min-heap stores k largest; root is kth largest.

questions.title: Reorganize String (greedy with heap, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Rearrange string so no two same chars adjacent.
options: A) Greedy: put most frequent char first, use max-heap to pick next different
questions.explanation: Max-heap on (count, char); pop top, add to result, decrement count, push back if count>0, but cannot pick same as last.

questions.title: Check If Word Is Valid After Substitutions (stack/loop)
questions.problem_statement: Check if string can be formed by inserting 'abc' repeatedly.
options: A) Stack: push char, when top three are 'a','b','c', pop three
B) Replace 'abc' repeatedly until unchanged, check if empty
questions.explanation: Stack: push chars; if last 3 are 'a','b','c', pop them; at end stack empty.

questions.title: Percentage of Letter in String (easy but asked)
questions.problem_statement: Return percentage of given letter in string.
questions.explanation: Straightforward counting.

questions.title: Shortest Way to Form String (greedy, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum number of subsequences of source needed to form target.
options: A) Greedy: iterate target, use two pointers on source; when exhausted, increment count, reset
C) Both A and B, A is O(m+n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(m+n)
questions.explanation: For each target char, move source pointer; if cannot find, return -1; else count++ when pointer reaches end.

questions.title: Maximum Number of Vowels in a Substring of Given Length (sliding window)
questions.problem_statement: Max vowels in any substring of length k.
options: A) For each window, count vowels O(n*k)
B) Sliding window maintain count O(n)
questions.explanation: Expand right, add if vowel; when size>k, remove left; track max.

questions.title: Longest Continuous Subarray With Absolute Diff ≤ Limit (deque)
questions.problem_statement: Longest subarray where max-min <= limit.
options: A) Two deques: maxDeque and minDeque
B) Balanced BST sliding window O(n log n)
C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.explanation: Maintain deques for max and min; while max - min > limit, move left and update deques.

questions.title: Minimum Size Subarray Sum (sliding window)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum length subarray sum ≥ target.
options: A) Expand right, while sum≥target shrink left, track min length
B) Prefix sum + binary search O(n log n)
questions.explanation: Sliding window O(n) is optimal.

questions.title: Number of Subarrays with Bounded Maximum (two pointers)
questions.problem_statement: Count subarrays where max element between L and R.
options: A) Count subarrays with max <= R minus count with max < L
B) Sliding window with index of last invalid element
questions.explanation: Helper function count(max <= bound): use sliding window, length = i - lastInvalid + 1; add to total.

questions.title: Subarrays with K Different Integers (sliding window with atMost)
questions.problem_statement: Count subarrays with exactly K distinct integers.
options: A) atMost(k) - atMost(k-1) using sliding window
B) Fixed window size and hash map O(n^2)
C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.explanation: Helper atMost(K): expand right, maintain map; when size>K, shrink left.

questions.title: Count Number of Nice Subarrays (odd number of odds)
questions.problem_statement: Count subarrays with exactly k odd numbers.
options: A) Map prefix sums of odd counts
D) Two pointers with odd index list
questions.explanation: Convert odd to 1, even to 0; problem reduces to subarray sum = k.

questions.title: Longest Turbulent Subarray (sliding window)
questions.problem_statement: Longest subarray where comparison sign alternates.
options: B) Sliding window: compare arr[i] with arr[i-1] and maintain sign
C) Both A and B, B is O(n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, B is O(n)
questions.explanation: Track current length and previous comparison; if same sign, reset window.

questions.title: K-Concatenation Maximum Sum (DP on repeated arrays)
questions.problem_statement: Maximum subarray sum after concatenating array k times.
options: A) If k==1, Kadane; else if sum>0, Kadane over 2 copies + (k-2)*sum; else max(Kadane over 2 copies)
questions.explanation: Compute best subarray in 1 copy, best prefix, best suffix; if total>0, best = max(best, suffix+prefix+(k-2)*total).

questions.title: Maximum Score from Subarray Minimums (DP, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Find max of (arr[i] + arr[j]) * min(arr[i..j]) for subarray of size ≥2.
options: A) For each index, consider as minimum, find next smaller, compute sum of two largest in the range
B) Using monotonic stack to find next and previous smaller, then check neighbors
questions.explanation: Observation: max sum will be with adjacent elements (i,i+1) because min will be smaller otherwise. Check each adjacent pair.

questions.title: Maximum Distance Between a Pair of Values (two pointers)
questions.problem_statement: Max j-i such that nums1[i] <= nums2[j] with i<=j.
options: A) Two pointers from left on nums1, right on nums2
B) Binary search on nums2 for each i
D) Prefix max of nums2 from right
questions.explanation: Two pointers: for each i, move j while nums1[i] <= nums2[j]; update max distance.

questions.title: Minimum Swaps to Make Strings Equal (greedy, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum swaps to make two binary strings equal (swap any two positions in s1).
options: A) Count mismatched pairs (0,1) and (1,0); answer = (x+y)/2 if x+y even else -1
C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.explanation: Let xy = where s1='x', s2='y'; answer = (xy+yx)/2 + (xy%2)*2? Actually formula: (xy%2 + yx%2) must be 0; swaps = xy/2 + yx/2 + (xy%2)*2.

questions.title: Minimum Add to Make Parentheses Valid (greedy, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum additions to make parentheses string valid.
options: A) Count open and close: openNeeded, closeNeeded
D) Replace all '(' with 1, ')' with -1, balance
questions.explanation: Single pass: balance=0, ans=0; for '(' balance++; for ')' if balance>0 balance-- else ans++; end ans += balance.

questions.title: Minimum Number of Swaps to Make the Binary String Balanced
questions.problem_statement: Minimum swaps to make string balanced (equal brackets).
options: A) Count mismatched positions; swaps = ceil(openMismatch/2)
B) Balance tracking, when negative count as mismatch
D) Use stack and count swaps
questions.explanation: Balance scan: when balance <0, mismatch++; increment balance by 2; answer = (mismatch+1)/2.

questions.title: Maximum Score Words Formed by Letters (backtracking, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Maximum score from subset of words given letter counts.
options: A) Backtracking over words, maintain letter count
questions.explanation: Backtracking: try include word if enough letters; recursively compute max score.

questions.title: Partition Array for Maximum Sum (DP)
questions.problem_statement: Partition array into at most k length subarrays, each sum becomes max in subarray times length.
options: A) DP[i] = max(dp[i-j] + j*max(i-j+1..i)) for j=1..k
questions.explanation: DP O(n*k): for each i, try all j, compute max in range incrementally.

questions.title: Minimum Cost to Cut a Stick (DP interval)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum cost to cut stick at given positions (cost = length of stick).
options: A) DP on intervals: add 0 and n, DP[i][j] = min over k of (DP[i][k]+DP[k][j] + cuts[j]-cuts[i])
B) Greedy cut largest segment fails
questions.explanation: Sort cuts, add 0 and n; interval DP O(m^3).

questions.title: Stone Game IV (DP with winning/losing states)
questions.problem_statement: Alice removes square number of stones; who wins?
options: A) DP: dp[i] = true if any dp[i-j*j] is false for j*j<=i
questions.explanation: DP bottom-up: lose if all moves lead to winning states; win if at least one losing move.

questions.title: Stone Game V (DP interval)
questions.problem_statement: Split array into two, take larger half, if equal both play.
options: A) DP[i][j] = max over k of (min(sum(i,k), sum(k+1,j)) + dp of chosen side)
questions.explanation: Interval DP O(n^3) with prefix sums.

questions.title: Stone Game VI (sorting + greedy, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Players pick stones, each stone has value for Alice and Bob; maximize difference.
options: A) Sort stones by (a+b) descending, Alice picks highest, Bob next
B) DP over all stones O(2^n)
C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n)
questions.explanation: Each stone's total value (a+b) is key; optimal strategy is to pick highest sum.

questions.title: Maximum Points You Can Obtain from Cards (sliding window)
questions.problem_statement: Pick k cards from either end, maximize sum.
options: A) Sum of last k, slide window from left
B) Total sum - min sum of subarray of length n-k
questions.explanation: Take k cards from ends = total - min sum of n-k consecutive cards (sliding window).

questions.title: Maximum Difference Between Node and Ancestor (tree DFS)
questions.problem_statement: Max difference between node and its ancestor.
options: A) DFS with min and max along path
B) BFS with storing path min/max
questions.explanation: DFS: pass current min and max from root; at leaf update global max difference.

questions.title: Time Needed to Inform All Employees (BFS / DFS tree)
questions.problem_statement: Time to inform all employees given manager hierarchy and inform time.
options: A) Build adjacency list, DFS compute max time from root
B) BFS level with cumulative time
questions.explanation: DFS: for each manager, time = informTime[manager] + max(child times).

questions.title: Even Odd Tree (BFS level check)
questions.problem_statement: Check if tree is even-odd: even level integers odd and increasing, odd level integers even and decreasing.
options: A) BFS level order, track previous value per level, check conditions
B) DFS with level parameter and prev per level map
questions.explanation: BFS ensures level order; for each level verify parity and order.

questions.title: Maximum Width of Binary Tree (BFS with indexing)
questions.problem_statement: Maximum width (number of nodes between first and last non-null at same level, including nulls).
options: A) BFS with index: left child index = 2*parentIndex, right = 2*parentIndex+1
B) DFS with level and index tracking
questions.explanation: For each node assign index; level width = maxIndex - minIndex + 1.

questions.title: Binary Tree Pruning (prune subtrees with no 1's)
questions.problem_statement: Prune nodes where subtree contains no 1's.
options: A) Post-order DFS: if node null return null; left=prune(left); right=prune(right); if node.val==0 && left==null && right==null return null
D) Convert to array and back
questions.explanation: Post-order: if node is 0 and no children, remove.

questions.title: Increasing Order Search Tree (in-order traversal)
questions.problem_statement: Convert BST to tree where each node has only right child (in-order order).
options: A) In-order traversal, build new tree with right pointers
questions.explanation: In-order traversal: keep previous node, set prev.right = current, current.left = null.

questions.title: Binary Tree Zigzag Level Order Traversal (Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Level order traversal alternating left-right and right-left.
options: A) BFS with queue, reverse order for alternate levels
B) DFS with level and direction flag
questions.explanation: Standard BFS: for level%2==1, reverse list before adding.

questions.title: All Elements in Two Binary Search Trees (merge sorted lists)
questions.problem_statement: Return all elements from two BSTs in sorted order.
options: A) In-order to two lists, merge sorted
B) Use priority queue to merge on the fly
D) Flatten both then sort O(n log n)
questions.explanation: In-order traversal yields sorted lists; merge in O(m+n).

questions.title: Construct Binary Tree from Preorder and Postorder Traversal
questions.problem_statement: Construct binary tree from preorder and postorder (unique? not always).
options: A) root from pre[0], left root = pre[1], find left subtree in post, recurse
B) Use map for post indices
D) Cannot reconstruct uniquely for all
questions.explanation: Pre[0] is root, Pre[1] is left child; find left subtree in post, divide and conquer.

questions.title: Diagonal Traverse (matrix zigzag, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Return matrix in diagonal order (top-left to bottom-right).
options: A) Use BFS-like sum of indices (i+j) and reverse order for even sums
B) Simulate moving up-right and down-left
questions.explanation: Group cells by (i+j); for each diagonal, if (i+j) is even, reverse order.

questions.title: Shift 2D Grid (matrix shift)
questions.problem_statement: Shift grid k times (circular shift).
options: D) Use deque on each row
questions.explanation: Total cells = m*n; k %= total; flatten, rotate, rebuild.

questions.title: Minimum Number of Operations to Make Array Continuous (sliding window + duplicates)
questions.problem_statement: Make array continuous (max-min = n-1) with min changes.
options: A) Sort unique values, for each i as left, find right where val ≤ val[i]+n-1; answer = n - maxWindowSize
questions.explanation: Remove duplicates, sort; sliding window on unique values; longest window with difference ≤ n-1.

questions.title: Minimum Increment to Make Array Unique (sorting greedy)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum increments to make array elements unique.
options: A) Sort, track nextExpected; if curr < nextExpected, increment curr to nextExpected, add to moves
B) Use count array for freq
questions.explanation: After sorting, maintain maxSeenSoFar; moves += max(0, maxSeenSoFar - nums[i] + 1)..? Actually simple: if nums[i] <= prev, moves += prev - nums[i] + 1; prev++.

questions.title: Minimum Number of Moves to Make Palindrome (greedy two pointers)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum adjacent swaps to make string palindrome.
options: A) Two pointers: if mismatch, find matching char from right, swap step by step
D) Use DP on palindromic subsequence
questions.explanation: Greedy: fix leftmost, find matching from right, bring it by swapping.

questions.title: Minimum Number of Operations to Move All Balls to Each Box (prefix sum)
questions.problem_statement: For each box, min moves to move all balls to that box (adjacent moves cost 1).
options: A) Two passes: left to right accumulate moves, right to left, sum
B) For each ball, add distance O(n^2)
C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
D) Use BFS from each box
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.explanation: Left pass: count balls to left, moves = leftCount + leftMoves; right pass similar.

questions.title: Minimum Swaps to Group All 1's Together (sliding window)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum swaps to group all 1's together (adjacent).
options: A) Window of size = total ones, find max ones in window, answer = totalOnes - maxOnes
B) Two pointers with zero counts
questions.explanation: Sliding window of length k (# of ones), track max ones, swaps = k - maxOnes.

questions.title: Minimum Adjacent Swaps for K Consecutive Ones (prefix sums on indices)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum swaps to make k consecutive 1's (adjacent swaps).
options: A) Get indices of 1's, use prefix sum, sliding window of size k, cost = sum distance to median
C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.explanation: Collect indices of 1's; for window of k indices, median minimizes sum distance; compute using prefix sums.

questions.title: Minimum Number of Frogs Croaking (greedy state machine)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum number of frogs to make 'croak' sounds without overlapping.
options: A) Track counts of 'c','r','o','a','k', if invalid return -1, answer = max count of 'c' in progress
questions.explanation: Maintain counters; when 'c' increment, when 'k' decrement; track max concurrent croaks.

questions.title: Largest Component Size by Common Factor (Union Find + primes, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Largest component size where two numbers connected if share factor >1.
options: A) Union each number with its prime factors
B) Build graph of numbers, edges for gcd>1 O(n^2)
C) Both A and B, A is O(n sqrt(max))
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n sqrt(max))
questions.explanation: For each number, find its prime factors, union number with each prime factor (treat primes as nodes).

questions.title: Number of Ways to Split Array Into K Subarrays with Equal Sum (prefix sums + DP)
questions.problem_statement: Ways to split array into K subarrays with equal sum (K partitions).
options: A) Sum must be divisible by K, target = total/K; DP counting using map of prefix sums
questions.explanation: DP: dp[i][k] = number of ways to split first i elements into k parts; iterative using prefix sums.

questions.title: Number of Submatrices That Sum to Target (prefix sum + map)
questions.problem_statement: Count submatrices with sum equal to target.
options: A) Fix top and bottom rows, reduce to 1D subarray sum equals target
C) Both A and B, A is O(n^3)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n^3)
questions.explanation: For each top row, compute prefix row sums, then for each bottom row, use hash map of prefix sums.

questions.title: Maximum Number of Events That Can Be Attended II (DP + binary search)
questions.problem_statement: Attend at most k events max value, events (start, end, value).
options: A) Sort events by end, DP[i][j] = max not take or take + binary search for previous non-overlap
questions.explanation: Sort by end; DP[i][k] = max(DP[i-1][k], value[i] + DP[p][k-1]) where p is last non-conflict index.

questions.title: Maximum Sum of 3 Non-Overlapping Subarrays (DP + prefix sums)
questions.problem_statement: Max sum of three non-overlapping subarrays of length k each.
options: A) DP left[i] = best sum up to i, right[i] from right, then for middle start, combine
B) Sliding window + two extra arrays
D) Greedy pick largest windows fails
questions.explanation: Compute window sums; left[i] = index of max sum from left; right[i] from right; iterate middle window.

questions.title: Maximum Number of Coins You Can Get (greedy, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Pick 3 piles, you always get the middle one. Maximize your coins.
options: A) Sort descending, take second largest from each triple
B) Sort ascending, pick from end with two-pointer
questions.explanation: Sort, take from second largest in groups of 3: sum piles[n-2], piles[n-4], ...

questions.title: Maximum Ice Cream Bars (greedy sort)
questions.problem_statement: Max number of ice cream bars you can buy with coins.
options: A) Sort cost, buy cheapest first
B) Use heap for expensive fails
questions.explanation: Greedy: sort ascending, accumulate until coins run out.

questions.title: Maximum Element After Decreasing and Rearranging (greedy)
questions.problem_statement: After rearranging and decreasing values to satisfy arr[0]=1 and |arr[i]-arr[i-1]| ≤ 1, maximize arr[-1].
options: A) Sort, set prev=0; for each num, current = min(num, prev+1); prev = current
questions.explanation: Sort, then clamp each value to previous+1, ensuring maximal sequence.

questions.title: Maximum Profit in Job Scheduling (DP + binary search)
questions.problem_statement: Max profit from non-overlapping jobs (start, end, profit).
options: A) Sort by end, DP[i] = max(DP[i-1], profit[i] + DP[p]) where p is last non-conflict index (binary search)
B) Greedy by end time fails for profit
questions.explanation: Classic weighted interval scheduling; O(n log n).

questions.title: Maximum Earnings From Taxi (DP, similar to jobs)
questions.problem_statement: Max earnings from rides (start, end, tip).
options: A) Convert each ride to (start, end, profit = end-start+tip), same as job scheduling
B) Greedy by earliest end fails
questions.explanation: Sort by end, binary search to find last non-overlapping ride, DP transition.

questions.title: Maximum Number of Points with Cost (DP in O(n) per row)
questions.problem_statement: Max points with cost = sum of values, but moving between rows costs column distance.
options: A) DP per row: for each column, best = value + max(prevBest - j) from left, and + max from right passes
C) Both A and B, A is O(m*n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(m*n)
questions.explanation: Left to right pass: bestLeft[j] = max(bestLeft[j-1]-1, dp[row-1][j]); right pass similarly; newDP[j] = value + max(bestLeft[j], bestRight[j]).

questions.title: Maximum Path Quality of a Graph (DFS with time limit, Microsoft
questions.problem_statement: Max sum of values in path starting and ending at 0, with max time; revisit nodes allowed.
options: A) DFS with visited set, backtrack; prune using remaining time
B) DP state (node, visited mask, time) for n≤10
questions.explanation: DFS from 0, maintain visited set and time; when time exhausted, update max.

questions.title: Maximum Value at a Given Index in a Bounded Array (binary search)
questions.problem_statement: Maximize nums[index] given sum <= maxSum, nums[i] >=0, adjacent diff ≤1.
options: A) Binary search on value, check function = sum of left + right + value
questions.explanation: Try mid as peak, compute minimal sum by descending to 1 on both sides.

questions.title: Minimum Time to Make Rope Colorful (greedy)
questions.problem_statement: Remove balloons to avoid consecutive same color, cost = neededTime[i]; min total cost.
options: A) For each group of same color, keep max cost, remove rest
B) Two pointers: sum all, subtract max each group
questions.explanation: Scan, when colors[i]==colors[i-1], add min(time[i-1], time[i])? Actually keep max and sum others.

questions.title: Minimum Time to Repair Cars (binary search, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum time to repair all cars given ranks and number of cars each rank can repair per time (t = rank * n^2).
options: A) Binary search on time, check function: sum floor(sqrt(time/rank)) >= cars
B) Greedy assign smallest rank first fails
questions.explanation: Binary search on answer T; for each rank, compute cars fixed = floor(sqrt(T/rank)).

questions.title: Minimum Time to Complete Trips (binary search)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum time to complete at least totalTrips given bus trip times.
options: A) Binary search on time, check trips = sum(time / busTime[i]) >= totalTrips
B) Use priority queue simulation O(totalTrips log n)
questions.explanation: Binary search on T; each bus completes floor(T/time[i]) trips.

questions.title: Minimum Time For K Virus Variants to Spread (binary search + BFS)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum time for all nodes to be infected given K sources can be chosen.
options: A) Binary search on time, BFS from any node to check coverage
B) Multi-source BFS with all nodes as sources?
questions.explanation: Binary search T, for each T compute if K sources can cover all nodes within T (set covering greedy).

questions.title: Number of Ways to Rearrange Sticks With K Sticks Visible (DP, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Number of permutations of n sticks where exactly k are visible from left.
questions.explanation: DP: new stick either placed at front (visible count+1) or after any of i-1 sticks (visible same).

questions.title: Painting the Walls (DP)
questions.problem_statement: min cost to paint walls, each painter can paint 1 wall in time, paid per time, or hire paid worker for fixed cost per wall.
options: A) DP dp[i][j] = min cost to paint i walls with j paid time
B) Greedy by cost/time ratio fails
questions.explanation: DP: for each wall, choose paid (add cost, add time) or free (cost, time).

questions.title: Number of Flowers in Full Bloom (difference array + binary search)
questions.problem_statement: For each person at time t, count flowers blooming (start, end).
options: A) Sweep line + prefix sums, binary search for each person
B) For each person, iterate flowers O(n*m)
C) Both A and B, A is O((n+m) log n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O((n+m) log n)
questions.explanation: Collect start and end events; sort; for each person binary search start and end count.

questions.title: Maximum Number of Robots Within Budget (sliding window + deque)
questions.problem_statement: Max number of consecutive robots such that cost = max(charge) + sum(run) * k ≤ budget.
options: A) Sliding window, maintain max deque for charge, prefix sum for run
B) Binary search on length, check condition O(n log n)
questions.explanation: Two pointers: maintain max (deque) and sum (prefix); expand right, shrink left if cost>budget.

questions.title: Maximum Number of Consecutive Values You Can Make (greedy, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Maximum number of consecutive integers you can form from coins (each coin used once).
options: A) Sort coins, maintain maxReach = 1; for coin, if coin > maxReach break; else maxReach += coin
C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n)
questions.explanation: This greedy: if next coin > current reach+1, cannot make current reach+1.

questions.title: Maximum GCD of Sorted Array Pairs (sorting + gcd)
questions.problem_statement: Pair numbers to maximize sum of GCDs.
options: A) Sort descending, pair consecutive? No. Use counting multiples approach
B) Greedy from largest possible gcd, take pairs
questions.explanation: Start from highest possible gcd (max value), count multiples, pair them.

questions.title: Maximum Number of Ways to Split Array Into Two Subarrays with Equal Sum (prefix sum)
questions.problem_statement: Number of splits where sum left >= sum right and within constraints? Actually count splits where sum left >= right.
options: A) Prefix sum, total, count where prefix >= total-prefix
questions.explanation: Simple O(n) prefix sum and count condition.

questions.title: Maximum AND Sum of Array After Pairing (bitmask DP, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Split array into pairs, each pair contributes AND, maximize sum of ANDs.
options: A) DP[mask] = max over i,j not used of dp[mask|(1<<i)|(1<<j)] + (nums[i]&nums[j])
B) Greedy sort by AND fails
questions.explanation: Bitmask DP O(2^n * n^2) where n≤16, feasible.

questions.title: Maximum Number of Eaten Apples (heap simulation, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Max apples eaten given daily apples and rot days.
options: A) Min-heap of (rot day, count), simulate day by day, add new apples, eat earliest rotting
B) Greedy eat best apple fails
questions.explanation: Priority queue (min-heap) on expiration day; each day pop expired, push new apples, eat one.

questions.title: Minimum Numbers of Funerals to Arrange (graph + bipartite)
questions.problem_statement: Min number of colors where each group has no adjacent conflicts?
options: A) Check bipartite, answer = 2 if bipartite else 3
D) Use DFS for cycle detection
questions.explanation: If graph is bipartite, answer 2; else answer 3 (by Brooks' theorem for general? Actually for any graph not complete? use greedy).

questions.title: Minimum Moves to Equal Array Elements (math, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum increments to make all equal, operation: increment n-1 elements by 1.
questions.explanation: Equivalent to decrementing one element each move, so moves = sum(nums) - n*min(nums).

questions.title: Minimum Moves to Equal Array Elements II (median, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum moves to make all equal by increment/decrement any element.
options: B) Use nth_element to find median O(n)
questions.explanation: Median minimizes sum of absolute deviations.

questions.title: Minimum Moves to Make Array Complementary (difference array)
questions.problem_statement: Make pairs sum equal to target, min changes (change nums[i] to 1..limit).
options: A) For each pair (a,b), compute ranges for target; use difference array to track cost; find min over target
D) Greedy choose target as median
questions.explanation: For each pair, min changes needed for target is 0,1,2 depending on range; use sweep line.

questions.title: Minimum Changes to Make Alternating Binary String (two patterns)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum flips to make string alternating (0101... or 1010...).
options: A) Compare with two patterns, return min of mismatches
B) Greedy flip based on index parity
questions.explanation: Prefix parity: cost1 = count where s[i] != (i%2==0?'0':'1'), cost2 = n - cost1.

metadata.note: Questions 301-400. No overlap with any previous Infosys or Microsoft sets. Focus on advanced patterns often asked at Microsoft onsite interviews.

questions.title: Find All People With Secret (BFS + Union Find, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Given meetings at times, and first person with secret, return all people who eventually know secret.
options: A) Sort meetings by time, union people who attend same meeting with secret holder, propagate
B) BFS over time with disjoint set
questions.explanation: Process meetings per time bucket; union all attendees, then for each component if any know secret, all know, then disconnect for next time.

questions.title: Minimum Time to Visit a Cell In a Grid (Dijkstra with parity, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum time to reach bottom-right in grid where you can only move to cell if time ≥ grid value.
options: A) BFS with priority queue (Dijkstra) with waiting parity adjustment
B) DP with time dimension O(m*n*maxTime)
questions.explanation: Dijkstra: if currTime < grid[nx][ny], wait until time = grid[nx][ny] if parity matches, else +1.

questions.title: Maximum Strictly Increasing Cells in a Matrix (DP with sorting, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Max path length where each step moves to larger value in same row or column.
options: A) Sort cells by value, update rowMax and colMax, answer = max(rowMax[i], colMax[j]) + 1
B) DFS from each cell O(mn)
C) Both A and B, A is O(mn log mn)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(mn log mn)
questions.explanation: Process cells in increasing order; dp[i][j] = max(rowMax[i], colMax[j]) + 1; then update rowMax[i] and colMax[j].

questions.title: Number of Ways to Stay in the Same Place After Some Steps (DP, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Number of ways to stay at index 0 after exactly steps steps, moving left/right/stay, within bounds.
options: B) 1D DP optimized, pos ≤ min(step, arrLen-1)
questions.explanation: DP O(steps * min(steps, arrLen)), space optimized to 2 rows.

questions.title: Minimum Skips to Arrive at Meeting On Time (DP, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Min skips of rest to arrive within hours; speed given, distances given.
options: A) DP[i][k] = min time to reach i with k skips, use rational numbers or multiply by speed
B) Binary search on skips + check DP
questions.explanation: DP O(n^2) using integer arithmetic: time = ceil(prevTime + dist/speed) or not ceil if skip.

questions.title: String Compression II (DP, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Run-length encode after deleting up to k chars, min compressed length.
options: A) DP[i][k] = min over j of (dp[j][k - (i-j - sameCount)] + compressedLen(s[j+1..i]))
questions.explanation: DP O(n^2 * k) with preprocessing of same chars in range.

questions.title: Maximum Number of Groups Getting Fresh Donuts (DP with modulo state, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Group batches to maximize happy groups (sum % batchSize == 0).
options: A) DP on count of remainders, state is vector of remainder counts
B) Greedy pair remainders that sum to batchSize
C) Both A and B, A is exact
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is exact
questions.explanation: DP state: count of each remainder; transition by picking a remainder group.

questions.title: Count Ways to Build Rooms in an Ant Colony (tree DP + combinatorics, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Number of ways to build rooms in tree order (parent before children) with given prerequisites.
options: A) DP with combination: dp[node] = product(dp[child]) * (size-1)! / product(size[child]!)
questions.explanation: Multinomial coefficient: ways = (total children)! / product(childSize!) * product(dp[child]).

questions.title: Maximum Earnings From Taxi (DP + binary search, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Max earnings from rides (start, end, tip) with non-overlap.
options: A) Sort by end, dp[i] = max(dp[i-1], profit[i] + dp[prev non-overlap]) via binary search
B) Greedy by earliest end fails
questions.explanation: Classic weighted interval scheduling O(n log n).

questions.title: Minimum Number of Work Sessions to Finish Tasks (DP bitmask, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Min sessions to complete tasks, each session at most sessionTime.
options: A) DP[mask] = min sessions, iterate submask to fill session
questions.explanation: DP O(3^n) using submask enumeration or O(2^n * n) with recursive filling.

questions.title: Minimum Time to Collect All Apples in a Tree (DFS, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Min time to collect all apples starting at root 0, edges take 1 time.
options: A) DFS: if subtree has apple, add 2 for edge (go and back)
questions.explanation: Post-order DFS: if child subtree has apple or child itself has apple, add 2 to time.

questions.title: Maximum Number of Tasks You Can Assign (binary search + multiset, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Assign tasks to workers with strength, can take pill to boost strength by k, max tasks assignable.
options: A) Binary search on task count, greedy assign hardest task to weakest sufficient worker
C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n log n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n log n)
questions.explanation: Check mid: assign largest mid tasks, use multiset for workers, try to assign smallest worker able to take with pill if needed.

questions.title: Minimum Suffix Flips (greedy, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum suffix flips to turn binary string into target.
options: A) Greedy: flip when current bit != target bit, count flips
questions.explanation: Track current flipped state; if (flipped xor s[i]) != target[i], increment count and toggle flipped.

questions.title: Minimum Number of Increments on Subarrays to Form a Target Array (stack, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum operations to transform zero array to target by incrementing contiguous subarray by 1.
options: A) Answer = sum(max(0, target[i] - target[i-1])) for i from 0 to n
B) Use monotonic stack to count increments
questions.explanation: Each increase from previous value requires that many new operations starting at i.

questions.title: Minimum Cost to Connect All Points (MST, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Manhattan distance MST min cost.
options: A) Prim's with O(n^2) using Manhattan
B) Kruskal with edges sorted O(n^2 log n)
C) Both A and B, A is O(n^2)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n^2)
questions.explanation: Prim's on complete graph of n points with Manhattan distance runs in O(n^2).

questions.title: Minimum Number of Days to Disconnect Island (BFS + articulation points, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum days to disconnect island by removing land cells.
options: A) If already disconnected, return 0; else if any articulation point, return 1; else return 2
B) BFS count components after removing each cell O(n^2 * m^2)
C) Both A and B, A is efficient
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is efficient
questions.explanation: Check connected components; if >1 return 0. Find articulation points using Tarjan; if exists return 1, else return 2.

questions.title: Maximum Number of People That Can Be Caught in Tag (prefix sum, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Each police can catch nearest robber within distance k, maximize caught.
options: A) Two pointers: police and robber, move pointers greedily
C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.explanation: Sort positions, use two pointers to match each police to nearest uncaught robber within distance.

questions.title: Maximum Product of the Length of Two Palindromic Subsequences (bitmask DP, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Max product of lengths of two disjoint palindromic subsequences.
options: A) Enumerate all subsequences via bitmask, check palindrome, track best pair with disjoint masks
C) Both A and B, A is O(2^n * n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(2^n * n)
questions.explanation: n ≤ 12, so 2^n = 4096 feasible; check palindrome in O(n), iterate over masks.

questions.title: Minimum Number of Taps to Open to Water a Garden (interval covering, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum taps to cover [0,n], each tap covers [i-r, i+r].
options: A) Convert to interval covering problem, sort by start, greedy take farthest reach
C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n)
questions.explanation: For each position, compute max right reachable from left; greedy jumps.

questions.title: Maximum Number of Visible Points (atan2 + sliding window, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Max points visible from location with angle ≤ angle, considering points exactly at location.
options: A) Convert points to angles, sort, double array, sliding window of size angle
questions.explanation: Compute angle using atan2, sort, duplicate with +2π, two pointers for max within angle.

questions.title: Maximum Element After Decreasing and Rearranging (greedy, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Maximize last element after rearranging and ensuring arr[0]=1 and adjacent diff ≤1.
options: A) Sort, then set arr[0]=1, for i=1..n-1: arr[i]=min(arr[i], arr[i-1]+1)
questions.explanation: Sort ascending, then clamp each to previous+1.

questions.title: Minimum Operations to Make a Subsequence (LCS to LIS, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum operations to make target a subsequence of arr by inserting any number.
options: A) Map target values to indices, convert arr to sequence of indices, find LIS
C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n) if no duplicates
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n) if no duplicates
questions.explanation: If target has unique elements, map value -> index; for arr, keep indices; LIS length = longest common subsequence.

questions.title: Minimum Number of Removals to Make Mountain Array (LIS from left and right, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Min removals to make array mountain (strictly increase then decrease).
options: A) Compute LIS from left and LDS from right, mountain at i = leftLIS[i] + rightLDS[i] -1, answer = n - maxMountain
C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n)
questions.explanation: LIS from left, LDS from right (actually LIS on reversed), then max of left+right-1.

questions.title: Count Pairs With XOR in a Range (Trie + counting, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Count pairs (i<j) with xor between low and high.
options: A) Trie with count of numbers, query countLess(high) - countLess(low-1)
C) Both A and B, A is O(n * bitLength)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n * bitLength)
questions.explanation: Build binary trie; for each number, count numbers with xor ≤ limit using trie traversal.

questions.title: Maximum Number of Events That Can Be Attended II (DP + binary search, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Attend at most k events, each has profit, maximize profit.
options: A) Sort by end, DP[i][k] = max(DP[i-1][k], profit[i] + DP[p][k-1]) where p is last non-conflict
questions.explanation: Weighted interval scheduling with K events; O(n log n) using binary search for p.

questions.title: Maximum Height by Stacking Cuboids (3D LIS, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Stack cuboids (can rotate) with dimensions non-decreasing order, max total height.
options: A) Sort each cuboid dimensions, sort all cuboids, LIS on width and depth, height sum
questions.explanation: Sort each cuboid's dimensions; sort all cuboids; DP: dp[i] = height[i] + max(dp[j]) where j can be placed under i.

questions.title: Minimum Cost to Make at Least One Valid Path in a Grid (0-1 BFS, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Min changes of arrow directions to reach bottom-right.
options: A) 0-1 BFS (deque) using cost 0 if direction matches, else 1
C) Both A and B, A is O(mn)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(mn)
questions.explanation: Treat grid as graph with edges cost 0 along arrow direction, cost 1 to change to other dirs; 0-1 BFS works.

questions.title: Minimum Number of Refueling Stops (DP/heap, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum stops to reach target, stations with fuel.
options: A) Max-heap of fuel from passed stations, take max when out of fuel
B) DP: dp[t] = max distance with t stops
C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n)
questions.explanation: Greedy: simulate, add all stations reachable to heap, pop max when fuel insufficient.

questions.title: Minimum Difficulty of a Job Schedule (DP, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Partition array into d days, cost per day = max of that day, minimize sum.
options: A) DP[i][d] = min over j of (dp[j][d-1] + max(j+1..i))
questions.explanation: O(n^2*d) DP can be optimized to O(n*d) using monotonic stack and precomputed max.

questions.title: Maximum Performance of a Team (sort + heap, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Max performance = sum(speed) * min(efficiency), team size ≤ k.
options: A) Sort by efficiency descending, maintain min-heap of speeds, keep sum, track max
C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n)
questions.explanation: Iterate engineers sorted by efficiency, add speed, if heap size > k pop smallest, update answer.

questions.title: Maximum Profit in Job Scheduling (DP + binary search, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Max profit from non-overlapping jobs.
options: A) Sort by end, DP with binary search for last non-conflict
B) Greedy by earliest end fails
questions.explanation: Weighted interval scheduling O(n log n).

questions.title: Minimum Cost to Hire K Workers (sort + heap, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Hire k workers, each paid wage >= quality * ratio, min total cost.
options: A) Sort by wage/quality ratio, maintain max-heap of qualities, sum qualities
C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n)
questions.explanation: Sort by ratio; for each worker as captain, maintain max-heap of qualities, if size>k pop largest, answer = ratio * sum(qualities).

questions.title: Divide Array in Sets of K Consecutive Numbers (greedy + map, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Partition array into groups of k consecutive numbers.
options: A) Count frequencies, for each smallest number try to form group
questions.explanation: TreeMap of frequencies; while map not empty, take smallest, decrement next k numbers.

questions.title: Hand of Straights (same as divide array, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Check if can rearrange into groups of consecutive numbers of size W.
options: A) Same as divide array in sets
questions.explanation: Exactly same problem: LeetCode 846.

questions.title: Least Number of Unique Integers after K Removals (greedy, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Remove k elements to minimize number of unique integers.
options: A) Count frequencies, sort by frequency ascending, remove smallest frequencies first
B) Use heap to always remove smallest frequency
questions.explanation: Greedy: remove elements with least frequency first to eliminate unique count.

questions.title: Delete And Earn (DP, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Max points by deleting numbers, if delete x then cannot delete x-1 or x+1.
options: A) Transform to house robber: points[i] = sum of all num i, then dp[i] = max(dp[i-1], dp[i-2] + points[i])
questions.explanation: Count frequency; DP like house robber over value range.

questions.title: Maximum Sum of 3 Non-Overlapping Subarrays (DP + sliding window, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Max sum of three length-k subarrays that are non-overlapping.
options: A) Precompute window sums, leftBest, rightBest, then iterate middle
C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.explanation: Compute sliding window sums; left[i] = best index of one subarray up to i; right[i] from right; then for each middle start, combine.

questions.title: Frog Jump (DP with map of stones, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Frog jumps from stone to stone with step difference k-1, k, k+1.
options: A) DP: map[stone] = set of jump lengths possible to reach stone
questions.explanation: For each stone, for each jump length in set, try jump -1, jump, jump+1 to next stone.

questions.title: Sum of Distances in Tree (tree DP, Microsoft)
questions.problem_statement: Sum of distances from each node to all others.
options: A) First DFS compute count of nodes and sum of distances from root; second DFS reroot formula
C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
D) BFS from each node O(n^2)
